text: Our organisation has a clearly articulated code of conduct that is regularly communicated to all employees and stakeholders.
Senior leadership consistently demonstrates commitment to ethical behaviour and sets a visible 'tone from the top' on integrity matters.
How does your organisation handle ethical leadership training for board members and senior executives?

options.label: No formal ethics training exists for leadership
Ad-hoc training is available but not mandatory
Annual mandatory ethics training is provided to all senior leaders
Comprehensive, ongoing ethics training is embedded in leadership development with measurable outcomes

text: What role does the board or governing body play in overseeing your organisation's integrity programme?

options.label: The board has no formal role in integrity oversight
The board receives occasional reports on integrity issues
A dedicated board committee reviews integrity matters on a quarterly basis
The board actively governs the integrity programme with dedicated resources, regular reporting, and strategic integration

text: Does your organisation have a formally established ethics committee or integrity officer with a clear mandate and reporting line to senior leadership?
Our organisation has a comprehensive anti-bribery and anti-corruption policy that covers all forms of corruption, including facilitation payments and political contributions.
All employees are required to complete regular conflict-of-interest declarations and are aware of the procedures for reporting potential conflicts.
How does your organisation conduct corruption risk assessments?

options.label: No corruption risk assessment process exists
Risk assessments are conducted informally or only when required by regulators
Systematic risk assessments are performed annually and cover key business areas
Continuous, data-driven corruption risk assessments are integrated into enterprise risk management with clear mitigation plans

text: What due diligence procedures does your organisation apply to third parties, business partners, and suppliers?

options.label: No formal due diligence process for third parties
Basic background checks are performed for major contracts only
Standardised due diligence is applied to all significant third-party relationships with documented procedures
Risk-based enhanced due diligence programme with ongoing monitoring, red-flag escalation, and contractual anti-corruption clauses

text: Does your organisation maintain a clear policy on gifts, hospitality, and entertainment with defined thresholds, approval processes, and a centralised register?
Our organisation proactively publishes financial statements, procurement data, and key performance indicators in accessible formats for public scrutiny.
Information about the organisation's decision-making processes, including policy development and regulatory actions, is made available to the public in a timely manner.
How does your organisation handle beneficial ownership transparency and disclosure of related-party transactions?

options.label: No formal disclosure requirements or processes exist
Basic disclosures are made when legally required but not proactively
Comprehensive beneficial ownership information and related-party transactions are regularly disclosed in public reports
Full transparency with real-time access to beneficial ownership registries, proactive disclosure exceeding legal requirements, and open-data formats

text: What level of transparency does your organisation maintain in its procurement and contracting processes?

options.label: Procurement processes are largely opaque with limited documentation
Major procurements follow formal procedures but details are not routinely published
All significant contracts are publicly tendered with results and award criteria published
End-to-end procurement transparency with open contracting data standards, published contracts, and real-time monitoring dashboards

text: Does your organisation publish an annual public integrity or transparency report that includes key metrics, identified risks, and improvement actions?
Our organisation provides multiple accessible channels (e.g., hotline, online portal, ombudsperson) for employees and external parties to report suspected misconduct.
Employees trust that they can report integrity concerns without fear of retaliation, and our anti-retaliation protections are well-communicated and enforced.
How does your organisation handle anonymous reports and protect the identity of whistleblowers?

options.label: Anonymous reporting is not available and reporter identities are not protected
Anonymous reporting is possible but there is no formal confidentiality protocol
Dedicated anonymous reporting channels exist with documented confidentiality procedures
Robust anonymous and confidential reporting system operated by an independent third party with strict data protection safeguards and regular audits

text: What investigation procedures follow when a whistleblower report is received?

options.label: No formal investigation procedures are in place
Investigations are conducted but lack standardised procedures and timelines
A documented investigation process exists with defined timelines, qualified investigators, and feedback to reporters
Independent, professionally managed investigations with legal oversight, defined escalation paths, outcome tracking, and systematic reporting to governance bodies

text: Does your organisation provide regular training to all staff on whistleblower rights, reporting procedures, and the EU Whistleblower Protection Directive requirements?
Our organisation conducts regular internal and external audits of its integrity and compliance programmes, with findings reported to senior management and governing bodies.
Clear disciplinary procedures are in place and consistently applied when integrity violations are confirmed, regardless of the seniority of the person involved.
How does your organisation monitor and track compliance with its integrity policies across departments and subsidiaries?

options.label: Periodic spot-checks are conducted but there is no centralised tracking system
A compliance monitoring programme with regular assessments, KPIs, and centralised incident tracking is operational
Integrated compliance management system with real-time dashboards, automated alerts, root-cause analysis, and continuous improvement cycles

text: When an integrity incident is identified, how does your organisation ensure corrective actions are implemented and their effectiveness verified?

options.label: Corrective actions are not systematically tracked or followed up
Actions are documented but follow-up depends on individual managers
A formal corrective action process exists with assigned responsibilities, deadlines, and management review
Comprehensive corrective action framework with root-cause analysis, effectiveness verification, lessons-learned integration, and governance-level reporting

text: Does your organisation maintain a centralised register of all integrity incidents, audit findings, and corrective actions that is reviewed by senior management at least quarterly?
Our organisation actively engages with civil society organisations, academic institutions, and community groups in the design and review of its integrity policies.
Feedback from citizens, service users, and external stakeholders is systematically collected and used to improve the organisation's integrity practices.
How does your organisation involve stakeholders in public consultation processes related to governance and integrity?

options.label: Consultations occur only when legally mandated with minimal outreach
Regular public consultations are conducted with documented responses to stakeholder input
Proactive, inclusive multi-stakeholder consultation processes with published feedback, impact assessments, and participatory decision-making mechanisms

text: What mechanisms does your organisation use to ensure meaningful participation of diverse communities in integrity-related decision-making?

options.label: No mechanisms for community participation in integrity matters
Occasional community meetings or surveys are held but results are not systematically integrated
Structured participation programmes exist with targeted outreach to underrepresented groups and published outcomes
Comprehensive participatory governance framework with citizen panels, co-design processes, accessibility measures, and transparent reporting on how input shaped decisions

text: Does your organisation maintain formal partnerships with civil society organisations or independent watchdog bodies to monitor and strengthen its integrity practices?
